seo.title: Building RAG Pipeline with Google ADK + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build rag pipeline with Google ADK + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building a RAG Pipeline with Google's Agent Development Kit (ADK) enables powerful document retrieval, vector search, and Gemini-powered synthesis for accurate responses. However, multi-agent delegation in ADK often leads to circular dependencies, model inconsistencies, and tool mismatches that undermine reliability. Vex provides essential guardrails to verify source faithfulness, detect hallucinations, and ensure citation accuracy in high-risk deployments.

architectureOverview.heading: RAG Pipeline Architecture with Google ADK
architectureOverview.description:
The RAG Pipeline in Google ADK typically features a Router Agent that receives user queries and delegates to specialized Retriever and Synthesizer Agents. The Retriever Agent performs vector search against a document corpus using embeddings, assembles relevant context chunks, and passes them to the Synthesizer Agent. The Synthesizer leverages Gemini models to generate responses with source attribution while maintaining conversation state across agents.

Agents communicate via ADK's session-based protocol, with tools handling embedding generation, vector search (e.g., via Vertex AI Vector Search), and response formatting. Long conversations risk state corruption as context windows expand, and multi-agent handoffs create dependency loops if not carefully orchestrated. Vex integrates as a before_agent_callback to validate inputs/outputs at each delegation point.

This architecture excels for enterprise knowledge bases but requires rigorous verification of retrieval relevance, synthesis faithfulness, and citation integrity to maintain trust.

risks.name: Multi-agent delegation creating circular dependencies
risks.description: Retriever Agent waiting for Synthesizer output while Synthesizer awaits refined retrieval creates infinite loops
risks.mitigation: Vex workflow validation prevents recursive agent calls and enforces delegation DAGs

risks.name: Gemini model switching causing output inconsistency
risks.description: Different agents using gemini-1.5-pro vs gemini-1.5-flash leads to format mismatches in citations
risks.mitigation: Vex standardizes response schemas and validates model-config consistency across agents

risks.name: Source faithfulness failures
risks.description: Synthesizer generates plausible but ungrounded claims not present in retrieved documents
risks.mitigation: Vex semantic similarity checks between response claims and source chunks

risks.name: Citation accuracy drift
risks.description: Agents fabricate or mismatch document IDs in attribution metadata during context re-assembly
risks.mitigation: Vex citation tracing verifies source-document mappings through agent handoffs

implementation.title: RAG Retriever Agent with Vector Search
implementation.description: Implements document retrieval using Vertex AI embeddings and vector search, formatting results for downstream synthesis
implementation.code:
from google.adk.agents import Agent
from google.cloud import aiplatform

retriever_agent = Agent(
    name="rag_retriever",
    model="gemini-1.5-flash",
    instruction="""You are a document retriever. Given a query:
    1. Generate embeddings using text-embedding-004
    2. Query vector index for top-5 matches
    3. Return JSON with source chunks and metadata
    4. Include document_id, chunk_id, score, and content
    Format: {'documents': [{'id': str, 'content': str, 'score': float}]}""",
    tools=[
        aiplatform.gapic.VectorSearchClient(),
        aiplatform.gapic.PredictionServiceClient()
    ]
)

def vector_search_tool(query: str):
    # Implementation using Vertex AI Vector Search
    index_endpoint = aiplatform.VectorSearchIndexEndpoint('projects/.../locations/.../indexEndpoints/...')
    results = index_endpoint.match(
        deployed_index_id='...', 
        queries=[query], 
        num_neighbors=5
    )
    return [{
        'id': r.id, 
        'content': r.data, 
        'score': r.distance
    } for r in results[0]]

implementation.title: RAG Synthesizer Agent with Citation Tracking
implementation.description: Synthesizes final response from retrieved context with source attribution and hallucination boundaries

implementation.title: Router Agent for Multi-Agent Orchestration
implementation.description: Coordinates query routing between retriever and synthesizer with session state management

implementation.title: Session State Manager for Long Conversations
implementation.description: Prevents context corruption by summarizing and pruning conversation history

vexIntegration.title: Vex before_agent_callback for Source Faithfulness
vexIntegration.description: Validates retrieved documents match query intent before synthesizer processing

vexIntegration.title: Hallucination Detection Wrapper
vexIntegration.description: Intercepts synthesizer outputs to detect and flag ungrounded claims

vexIntegration.title: Citation Accuracy Validator
vexIntegration.description: Ensures all citations trace back to actual retrieved documents with correct metadata

cta.heading: Secure Your Google ADK RAG Pipeline
cta.description: Deploy Vex guardrails today to achieve production-grade reliability for your enterprise RAG applications.
